summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates that ReadFileTool and WriteFileTool can read and write arbitrary files on the system without path validation. The exploit successfully read /etc/passwd and created a file at /tmp/pwned_by_filetool.txt, confirming the vulnerability.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate: (1) Reading /etc/passwd through ReadFileTool, proving arbitrary file read capability, and (2) Writing a file to /tmp, proving arbitrary file write capability.
actual_observed_result: The exploit successfully read /etc/passwd using ReadFileTool without path validation, and created a malicious file at /tmp/pwned_by_filetool.txt using WriteFileTool. Both operations succeeded without access control checks.
verification_method: The run script verifies: (1) /etc/passwd contents contain expected system user entries ('root:' and '/bin/bash'), and (2) The written file exists at the arbitrary path with the controlled content. Both checks passed, confirming the vulnerability.
evidence_produced_by_run: ReadFileTool successfully read /etc/passwd without path validation
The file contents included expected system user entries proving actual file access
WriteFileTool created a file at /tmp/pwned_by_filetool.txt without path validation
The created file contained the attacker-controlled content
No access control or allowlist was enforced by either tool

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the vulnerable version, and mounts it into the container at /target-repo.
reproduction.pinned_commit_or_ref: main branch (vulnerable at time of audit)
